message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): sleep_with_cx observes budget deadline (tick 382)

Added `sleep_with_cx_observes_budget_deadline` — pins the tick-331
doc comment claim that `sleep_with_cx` observes cx budget deadline
(via `asupersync::time::budget_sleep`).

Test setup:
1. Build cx with Budget::new().with_deadline(Time::ZERO) — budget
   already exhausted at construction.
2. Call sleep_with_cx(&cx, Duration::from_secs(30)) — requested
   duration far exceeds the (elapsed) budget deadline.
3. Assert: returns Err, elapsed < 5s (sleep short-circuits instead
   of blocking for 30 seconds).

Why this matters: tick 331's doc extension on sleep_with_cx claimed
budget observation but never tested it. A future refactor that
removed budget_sleep's short-circuit logic would regress silently
— this test fires at build-if-runtime time.

Complements tick 382's distributed-HTTP-client-level snapshot
(tick 378 body `distributed_http_client_with_expired_budget_does_not_hang`):
that pins budget-aware behavior at the HTTP client layer, this pins
it at the foundational primitive layer.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 18s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
